channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sara Duterte Impeachment Hearing Continues Despite Absence
item.description: The House of Representatives’ justice committee proceeded with the formal impeachment hearing of Vice President Sara Duterte on March 25, 2026, despite her legal team's notice of non-attendance. The p...

item.title: Bannon Proposes ICE Deployment at Airports as Trial for 2026 Midterms
item.description: Steve Bannon, former White House strategist, has raised concerns about the role of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) in future elections. Speaking on his podcast 'War Room,' Bannon sugges...

item.title: Kenyatta National Hospital Calls on Families to Collect 480 Unclaimed Bodies Within a Week
item.description: Kenyatta National Hospital (KNH) in Nairobi has released a public notice giving families seven days to identify and collect 480 unclaimed bodies lying in its mortuary. The hospital stated that if no o...

item.title: Pride Festivals Request $3M from Ottawa as Corporate Support Wanes Amid Backlash
item.description: Pride festivals across Canada are seeking $3 million in annual funding from the federal government to bridge the gap left by a retreating corporate sponsorship base. As many companies scale back their...

item.title: The Hidden Costs and Risks of Shared Ownership Homes
item.description: Shared ownership, a government-backed housing scheme aimed at making home ownership accessible, has left many buyers facing rising service charges, financial pressures, and in some cases, trapped in u...

item.title: Crispin Odey Defends Himself in Tribunal Over Misconduct Allegations in UK Finance Ban Case
item.description: Former hedge fund manager Crispin Odey, 67, is contesting his ban from the UK financial sector in a tribunal after being accused of inappropriate conduct on at least 46 occasions between 2003 and 2020...

item.title: Australia Faces Rising Oil Prices Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: Australia is confronting a significant energy challenge as global oil prices surge due to supply blockages at the Strait of Hormuz, linked to the ongoing US and Israel conflict with Iran. Current onsh...

item.title: Kenyatta National Hospital Lists 102 Unclaimed Bodies with Seven-Day Collection Notice
item.description: Kenyatta National Hospital (KNH) in Nairobi has made a public announcement listing 102 unclaimed bodies currently in its Farewell Home mortuary. Relatives and members of the public have been given a s...

item.title: Justice Salami Criticizes Obi’s Eligibility for 2023 Presidential Election
item.description: Isa Ayo Salami, former President of the Court of Appeal, has publicly stated that Peter Obi should not have been allowed to run in the 2023 presidential election under the Labour Party. According to S...

item.title: Liberals to discuss potential age limits for social media platforms and AI chatbots
item.description: The Canadian government is set to hold discussions about imposing age restrictions on social media platforms and AI-powered chatbots. This debate is part of a larger conversation on how to better prot...

item.title: Construction of Kahama–Bulyanhulu Junction–Kakola Road Set to Ease Transport in Shinyanga Region
item.description: The construction of the 73-kilometer Kahama–Bulyanhulu Junction–Kakola road is currently in the asphalt-laying stage, with work progressing at 65% completion. The Sh101.2 billion project aims to enhan...

item.title: Disturbing Discovery in Kericho: 33 Bodies, Including 25 Children, Unearthed from Mass Grave
item.description: The discovery of a mass grave in Kericho has shocked Kenyans, with 33 bodies unearthed, including 25 children. The bodies, found at Makaburini Cemetery, were reportedly in advanced stages of decomposi...

item.title: Delay in NHS reporting of meningitis outbreak criticised
item.description: The NHS faced criticism after a two-day delay in reporting a meningitis case at the Queen Elizabeth the Queen Mother Hospital in Margate. The UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) was first notified on Fr...

item.title: Investigators examine shared responsibility in LaGuardia Air Canada runway collision
item.description: Investigators are examining multiple factors behind Sunday night's fatal collision between an Air Canada-operated CRJ-900 and a fire truck at New York's LaGuardia Airport. The crash killed both pilots...

item.title: Ongoing Developments in the Middle East Conflict as of March 25, 2026
item.description: The Middle East continues to face heightened tensions as of March 25, 2026, with significant geopolitical implications. Recent events have drawn international attention due to escalations involving th...

item.title: Abuja Court Finally Returns Sowore’s Passport After Cybercrime Case Dismissal
item.description: Human rights activist and former presidential candidate Omoyele Sowore don finally get him international passport back after more than one year wey e dey with court because of one cybercrime case wey ...

item.title: USMCA compliance offers limited relief as Canadian small businesses face U.S. tariffs
item.description: Many Canadian small businesses are struggling despite complying with the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA). Products that meet USMCA requirements should, in theory, be exempt from most U.S...

item.title: Ed Miliband explores reforms to decouple gas and electricity pricing in bid to cut household bills
item.description: Energy Secretary Ed Miliband has told Labour MPs that he is examining ways to separate electricity prices from gas costs to help lower household energy bills. Speaking to the Parliamentary Labour Part...

item.title: Tanzania explores using Air Tanzania cargo flights to sustain meat exports affected by Middle East conflict
item.description: Tanzania is taking steps to safeguard its growing meat export industry after flight disruptions caused by tensions in the Middle East led to a sharp fall in export volumes. The Tanzania Meat Board rev...

item.title: Supreme Court Justice Singh Shares Personal Journey Battling Cancer and Continuing Service
item.description: Supreme Court Associate Justice Maria Filomena Singh openly shared her personal experience battling cancer during the 1st Supreme Court Distinguished Women’s Forum on March 24, 2026. Appointed to the ...
